summary: Bounding pulses are a hallmark sign indicating an open PDA, whereas neonatal necrotizing enterocolitis affects premature babies generally without specific relation to PDA pathology; carbon monoxide washout suggests closed state contrary to what we expect for ongoing PDA; pulmonary hemorrhage may result indirectly as part of broader conditions influenced by PDA yet not its primary manifestation leading us conclude that \(\boxed{\text{carbon monoxide washout}}\) is least related given these considerations about each condition relative to true nature & impact of having persistently open PDA.

Answer choice: A
